The present invention relates to data management, and more particularly relates to an apparatus and method for providing data management to a distributed computer system.
Companies and other entities often rely on computer systems to store, modify, distribute and report their data. These types of systems can be organized using various levels of hierarchy, such as in a centralized computer system or in multiple decentralized computer systems, each with its own computer users or programs. Many modern database systems are used to manage data records and relationships across distributed computer systems. An example of such a database system is a database system built using the Oracle database management system, such as Oracle 9i, available from Oracle Corp.
A problem exists in that many current database systems cannot adapt to distributed environments. For example, many databases store data in tables. When such a database is distributed across multiple computer systems, the data stored in the database becomes stale. Such a problem exists because of the nature of many database systems for managing data records and relationships. While storing data within a database, a database administrator may change data stored in the database in ways that are unknown by those performing operations on the database. This is a problem because the changes made to the database are not properly propagated by the database to the operating system of each computer system used by

The present invention relates to a method and an apparatus for performing magnetic recording on a magnetic recording medium.
In magnetic recording devices, particularly in video tape recorders and digital VTRs, a great deal of effort is dedicated to make the magnetic recording medium stable and uniform in its magnetic characteristics. A magnetic layer of the recording medium is made thin, so that the layer can be more easily affected and thus more easily affected by the head and head assembly when being recorded with the head. When the track width becomes wide as the recording medium is being produced, however, the medium cannot be made uniform in thickness because the width of the head can not follow the track narrows. Further, a magnetic recording medium of the related art, because of the thickness of the magnetic layer, is often liable to be flaked (hereafter, “flaked condition”) as a result of the frequent use or vibration of the magnetic medium. To reduce or prevent the flaking phenomenon, U.S. Pat. The impact of Brexit will be devastating for public health and the NHS, which will struggle to recruit the staff needed to deliver services, the health service said in a new report on the consequences of a disorderly exit by Boris Johnson. It is expected to be the first publication of the Department of Health on the NHS facing the effects of a no-deal Brexit, following a request for data from the Department of Health in March.

Such technologies may include a variety of products and/or systems designed to provide information about light to a particular destination. In the telecommunication industry, optical transmission of data, images and video images using optical fibers as the principal transmission medium is a key element of information exchange. In the medical and scientific fields, microscopes often are used to observe samples such as biological structures. Optical microscopy systems commonly used in biomedical fields include high-end systems, such as the Eclipse Nikon Microscope Systems from Nikon Corporation. Similarly, lasers and cameras are widely used in various biomedical fields, and there are many other other fields that use various types of sensors and/or other optical equipment and systems. Further, there are other fields that use similar equipment and systems.
Optical microscopy is commonly used to inspect samples, e.g., biological and chemical structures. However, existing optical microscopy systems and microscopes have a number of limitations. For example, existing optical microscopes are limited by their fields of view and thus are incapable of inspecting large and complex structures in which objects are arranged in an irregular way (i.e., a non-rectangular fashion). The field of the invention is optical imaging systems, and is generally described with reference to systems that use a light source to generate an image on an imaging screen. However, certain embodiments may be applied to any type of imaging device.
Many digital imaging systems use a linear array of individually controllable mirrors, or deformable mirrors, to deflect and combine a linear array of light sources. These systems typically include a light source, an imaging device such as an imager array which is coupled to the linear array, and a display for viewing the image at the imager array. The display is typically an LCD display, for example.
Conventional digital imaging systems typically also use a rotating shutter or aperture device to control the display or imager arrays. This technique is used to provide a continuous image at the imager array, because the aperture is not a perfect image mask when the system is in a dark state because of the finite size of the display or imager display area. This is described, for example, with reference to FIG. 1.

Catherine of Sweden

Catherine of Sweden (, ) (February 1, 1660 – May 23, 1720), was queen of Sweden, Norway and Finland by marriage to King Charles XII of Sweden. She was the daughter of King Gustavus of Sweden and Victoria of Pomerania.

Catherine was a child at the Battle of Poltava. She fled Sweden with her infant daughter and the royal family lived in exile. Catherine returned to Sweden in 1696 and succeeded her father to the throne in 1718. Catherine was the mother of King Charles XII of Sweden, by whom she was named queen consort of Sweden on June 10, 1718.   

She was the mother of  Christian VIII of Denmark (by her first marriage to his uncle, Ulrik Frederik of Denmark, who was King Charles I's favorite), of King Peter I of Portugal (by her second marriage to her second husband), and of King Frederick I of Prussia (by her first marriage to his aunt, Christiane Ulrikke, who was the wife of the Prussian Prince Frederick. She was also Duchess of Brabant and Holland during the minority of her grandson King Frederick I of Prussia, and Countess of Häls

Q:

Can anyone explain the logic behind 'f(x,y) = x+y'?

'f(x,y) = x+y' can be interpreted as 'y is the sum of x and y'. Can anybody tell me the logic behind such a solution. Why not just 'x + y = y?'

A:

In many programming languages, addition is commutative; that is, $x + y \neq x + z$ if $x \neq z$.  But f(x,y) cannot be interpreted as (a) $x + y$ or (b) $y + x$.  Those are, indeed, correct expressions, because $x + z = z + x = z$, in either case.
Instead, f(x,y) is equivalent to (a’) $y = x + y$ and (b’) $x = x + y$, where ’ denotes interpretation as if $x = x \neq z$, in which case we have to use a”.  In other words, we interpret f as if $x = y$, but then (b’), since $x = z

Q:

Getting errors when updating app in the App Store?

I've just followed the instructions here to build my app using the 'Xcode 6 beta 5' but I'm getting errors at the moment when trying to upload it:

An alert has been displayed to the developer that the app can't be processed due to an unknown
  error. Error: Unable to save the contents of application
  'Fantome'.

I've tried removing and re-adding the app and updating it with the same problem occurs. Can anyone please help? TIA,

A:

The issue is that the app you're trying to submit doesn't exist in the target directory for the app itself. The build directory is where Xcode is saving the binaries of your app to be used for submission.
Make sure you've set the app to be the main app for your target. If you're not sure which one it is, open up your target organizer window (⌘+1), go to Project Navigator and expand "Your App". There should be a field next to it that will have the name of the target. If it's called "Fantome", you're fine.

In many games, whether or not a player is using computer or a mobile device, it is very desirable to be able to check which players were using each game. For a mobile game, it is important to be able to identify whether a player is using the game with other players, using a controller or a tablet, or having a different mobile device.
There are two fundamental reasons why it is valuable to be able to easily check the game state or the state of use or lack of use. On the one hand, if a match has some special effects such as match duration or score, it would be of tremendous advantage if the game could be configured such that, among other measures, the match states or game states could be detected quickly, and this detection should be done without having to open the game or otherwise change the configuration of certain game parameters.
On the other hand, a player may wish only to know whether the player in possession of the controller was also using the game with that controller, or whether he was using a different device that was not associated with the controller.
The present disclosure solves the above-cited problems and satisfies the aforementioned criteria, namely, the ability to identify whether a game is being played by either party or using two or more partners

Why are there no answers that contain a code example instead of just a short comment?

I am wondering, why are there no code examples in the answers of question. While the code example in the questions have some explanation why it is not accepted, but code examples in the answers have no explanation why the answer is not accepted. The code example in the code examples is not enough for me to understand why the code is not accepted (but sometimes, it doesn't help me to understand why the code example in the answer is not accepted).
I understand that code example in the question is also not accepted to make the answer more readable, but if there are no code examples in questions, why there are no code examples in answers or why there is no explanation why the answer is not accepted?
Edit
To remove confusion, I am referring only to the answers and not to question that is already rejected. For example, there used to be an answer "Add X to Y" where the explanation was "It is not clear what the intention of 'and' is". So the answer was not accepted, then it was re-posted with some clarification. However, the new version of the answer became unaccepted.

The present invention relates to an exposure apparatus for performing an exposure of a circuit pattern on a substrate coated with a resist film. Specifically, the present invention relates to an exposure apparatus, in which a scanning optical system is a liquid optical system, the light which has passed through the liquid optical system is converged through a reflecting optical system and then an intermediate image of a pattern formed on a mask is formed on a substrate surface.
An exposure apparatus is one of the essential parts in a production process, which is indispensable for the mass production of a liquid crystal display panel, a semiconductor device or the like.
Typically, the exposure apparatus comprises a plurality of mask stages, a plurality of wafer stages and a plurality of optical units. The intermediate image of the circuit pattern formed on a mask with an aperture formed therein is projected onto a light projection surface of a wafer through a projection optical system. The intermediate image is formed on the wafer in accordance with each of various patterns by the light which has passed through an exposing lens of the projection optical system and a reflecting optical system, a projection lens and other elements.

SELECT * FROM C WHERE user NOT IN (id, user_info)

I want it in a single query, so the order doesn't matter (I have around 15000 tickets and 300 users).

How about this?

Moreover, these figures seem to be higher in certain segments of the population. It has been shown that, after excluding subjects on anti-retroviral treatment, tobacco smokers are associated with higher viral loads and greater risk of progression to opportunistic and primary AIDS manifestations. Q:

Is there a free tool to get a rough estimation of the time/disk IO in a Linux server (Ubuntu)?

For example the disk is 30GB and there is 15 minutes (15 min for each hour/day...) of use, is there a free tool like the one below?

If not what should I use ?
(Free tool for rough estimation would be acceptable although the answers should be accurate)

A:

If your goal is 'get an estimate' as in: "how many gigs of this file will be used, at best this is not a precise estimate.
If your goal is 'get a rough value' as in "how much will a 30 GB file be used in the month of July" then you have much better tools to calculate.
For example, the command lsof can show you the disk activity of a process, but you can't even guess the amount.  If you want to track the amount of an entire file, you can use ftruncate (which updates the size in every access) to get a close estimation even if the file isn't opened regularly.
If you want to track disk activity over a longer period of time, consider cron

The impact of a community-based programme on children's knowledge and practices regarding sexually transmitted infections.
This study evaluated the impact of a community-based school-based curriculum on children's knowledge and practices regarding sexually transmitted infections (STIs), in the absence of the disease. Using surveys and a pre-test before the intervention, a total of 1725 children, aged 11-12 years, participated in the intervention in grades 1, 5, and 7. The control group, in contrast, received their usual school curriculum. Children attending the intervention demonstrated a significant improvement in their knowledge about STIs with respect to the risks of cervical infection and HIV/AIDS. They also demonstrated increased knowledge about prevention techniques, such as washing with soap, having an HIV-negative mother, and oral, vaginal and anal sexual intercourse. Most participants also reported practicing safer sex. These gains were not influenced by the school, which remained unchanged. Thus, the intervention programme significantly increased children's knowledge about STIs as a public health problem and provided them with knowledge concerning the risks associated with multiple sexual partnerships and other transmission routes. The intervention programme provided a framework for children to make health decisions in an effective way.

AUSTIN (KXAN) — Police say they found more than a quarter-million dollars stuffed in the trunk of a car being used as a getaway to rob a convenience store.

They say on Monday, officers responded to the scene of the robbery in the 9500 block of Old Settler.

Officers were able to stop the getaway car in the 100 block of East Main Street that was allegedly used in the crime.

Officers said they found a black Toyota Corolla being used to pull over to a red light. Inside the car investigators found two suspects.

During the stop, officers found 20 bundles of money stuffed into the trunk of the Toyota Corolla. They said one of the suspects is suspected of being the driver in the crime.

Investigators say after checking video surveillance of the incident, they found it took over 40 police officers from the Austin Police Department and Dallas County jail, along with eight K-9 units into the crime scene on East Main Street.

Two suspects were taken into custody along with the Toyota Corolla as well as the vehicle used as a getaway vehicle. They are both listed in serious but non-criminal custody. The suspects are facing a total of 38 charges.

Q:

Why do you specify the `default` keyword on the class level in python?

In python, the syntax is often used for class definitions like this:
class myClass:
    default = 'default'

If you're not passing any argument to the default keyword in the class definition, what does it do in Python? Is it just a documentation that's redundant? I see it in some c extensions. 

A:

Default is a special variable that means "the default initializer for the name if the variable does not have an explicit default." It is an optional name argument that can be used to provide an initializer of a parameter that has an implied default of 0.
(The optional parens are not used; the name myClass is used when the class isn't provided.)

A:

Default keyword has two purposes:

It allows you to define the argument name which is being called upon if the variable is not supplied;
It allows defining the initializer which will be used if the variable is not supplied.

The goal of plant breeding is to combine in a single variety or hybrid various desirable traits. For field crops, these traits may include resistance to diseases and insects, tolerance to heat and drought, reducing the time to crop maturity, greater yield, and better agronomic quality. With mechanical harvesting of many crops, uniformity of plant characteristics such as germination and stand establishment, growth rate, maturity, and plant and ear height, is important.
Field crops are bred through techniques that take advantage of the plant's method of pollination. A plant is self-pollinated if pollen from one flower is transferred to the same or another flower of the same plant. A plant is cross-pollinated if the pollen comes from a flower on a different plant.
Plants that have been self-pollinated and selected for type for many generations become homozygous at almost all gene loci and produce a uniform population of homozygous plants that may be heterozygous for many loci. A cross between two plants each heterozygous at a number of gene loci produces a uniform population of heterozygous intergeneric hybrid plants that may be heterozygous for many gene loci. [Prevalence of human papillomavirus infections among women and men in Chile].
Human papillomavirus (HPV) is strongly associated with cervical cancer and genital warts. Despite high prevalence of HPV in developing countries, studies comparing prevalence in developed countries, between different ethnic populations, and among women and men at an individual level is scarce. Here we present the results of a survey of HPV infection in Chile which included 852 randomly selected women between 30 and 59 years, and 853 randomly selected men between 20 and 70 years. The prevalence of HPV infection was 4.5% for women and 3.7% for men. Infections by high-risk high-risk HPV genotypes were 1.8% for women and 1.4% for men. The majority of these infections were caused by HPV16, 33, 45, 51, 52, 70, 83, 85 and oncogenic types. In conclusion, our findings confirm lower frequency of infection by high-risk HPVs among Chilean women compared with other Latin American populations. Moreover, there was no difference between men and women in the prevalence of HPV infection and HPV genotypes. A randomized trial comparing an indwelling pleural catheter versus an endotracheal tube in a single anesthetic.
A prospective randomized study was undertaken in 30 patients undergoing abdominal surgery of anesthetic type and duration. Patients were randomly divided into two groups. There were two methods of anesthesia: endotracheal intubation in the first group (Group I), under general anesthesia with thiopental 0.5 mg and nitrous oxide 50% in 50% oxygen for 30 minutes. In the second group anesthesia was by intubated pleural catheter, with thiopental 0.5 mg and nitrous oxide 50% in 60% oxygen for 30 minutes. The time for induction of both methods (30 minutes) was comparable. The time for intubation was shorter in the first group. The incidence of emphysema was lower in the first group.

Q:

C/C++ error "warning: nonzero result from unary minus" with negative values and negative indexing

I'm having this issue:
warning: nonzero result from unary minus: 0.45 - -15

What is the error here? Why is this happening and how can I fix it? 
Here's the relevant piece of code:
int value=15;
value -= -15;

A:

value is a floating point value, so in effect you are subtracting 15 from itself, which is a perfectly valid operation. 
Also as @Alexey Ponomarev said,  the sign of value -(-15) is the same as -(15) - 15 = 0.45.

"This is just a warning to say you can't do that again," said a worker who gave birth in a New York hospital where she had a tubal ligation and other medical procedures. "This is how we do it -- you should have no other options," she said of the hospital. "As long as your baby is healthy, and they take care of you after you're out, then they should."

The worker's case is typical of the hospital's decision to remove a woman's tubes after the birth of a child, which is becoming an increasingly common and lucrative procedure. In this procedure, a section of a woman's fallopian tube -- an important passage used to make and conceive multiple babies -- is cut.

They did not, however, have their own separate HIV testing clinics. He received intravenous antibiotics for 2 days with amoxicillin, but the symptoms were not improved. Prenatal diagnosis of renal dysplasia in twins with short stature: a diagnostic problem.
Evaluation of renal status in twins with short stature is not always straightforward. The study includes a total of 27 patients referred for ultrasound at 28th gestational weeks who were in the normal range according to the international standards. The fetuses in the study were all found to be in trisomy 21 by the amniocentesis. Six of them were diagnosed with renal dysplasia with the aid of ultrasound examinations. At delivery and postnatally they all had unilateral dysplastic kidneys with dysplastic ventricular septum. The only case of monosomy 21 (21/21) was a twin with short stature, anencephaly and renal dysplasia. All the other monosomic fetuses were delivered and their kidneys did not show any dysplasia in the post-natal assessment. An ultrasonographic screening program for twin-pondering mothers to diagnose any dysplasia in the fetuses is important to avoid serious complications.

The best way to find out what’s going on on the internet, for the better or the worse, is to take a look at news websites (if you have the time and/or inclination to do so) and the comments sections therein. This is not always an easy task to accomplish accurately, especially when it involves comments in a language you cannot read, or when the comments are not written or spoken in English, not to mention when you find yourself immersed in debates on a very complex problem where there is likely to be a range of opinions and perspectives.

The most common mistakes to be wary of are:

Frequent misspellings/inconsistencies/mistakes in syntax/language

Unjustifiable generalizations (eg. about the size of the EU)

Inaccurate and irrelevant language. This sometimes requires close analysis, but is still the primary cause of many false accusations.

I am of the opinion that most comments and postings on blogs, newspapers, etc. are, at heart, personal opinion/opinion in context. This isn’t always the same opinion. In many cases there is a great many of them, and this is what I would want to consider when judging what I see

Prenatal ultrasound screening for neural tube defects.
Neural tube defects (NTDs) affect an estimated 1 of every 1,000 live births and cause significant mortality and morbidity. Despite an early and effective treatment in cases of anencephaly, many cases remain undetected until after birth. A prospective audit of ultrasound fetal growth parameters (head circumference and biparietal diameter) was undertaken at the St James' Royal Infirmary, Dublin, in order to confirm and supplement information supplied by the screening test (second trimester combined test using foetal fibronectin and nuchal translucency). Data from 1,085 singletons were received by 31st March 1999. In this period only seven were confirmed to have a NTD. Two cases of spina bifida, two of anencephaly and four of encephalocele were reported. There were two cases of dysraphic and two of polymicrogyria. The prevalence of NTDs in this population was 11.7 per 1,000 live singleton infants.

Early years

CBC Television program host

The present invention relates generally to microprocessors, and more particularly, to a system and method for updating a processor of the processor with power-on and other timing parameters in accordance with an external reference clock signal.
In a central processing unit (CPU) of a microprocessor the operating frequency thereof is usually increased gradually over a period of time to cope with increased performance requirement of the CPU. In a digital system having a high performance requirement, a digital signal processor (DSP) is preferably integrated with the microprocessor to meet the high performance requirement. In such a DSP, a clock signal having a high frequency is generated at a DSP clock generating circuit of the microprocessor or at an external clock signal source from the microprocessor of the digital system by means of a phase locked loop or a delay locked loop. The internal reference of the DSP is then locked with the phase locked loop or the delay locked loop at a specific frequency. Q:

Prolog select all elements between two elements

Given two variables with values a,b, I'd like to query all values between them, e.g. 
[a,a,b,a,b,a...a,b].

In other words: I'm looking for the set of elements from a to b, where no such elements exist.
How would that be possible with select?

A:

The solution is rather simple: first you should select all the elements that are not a (for instance: [a,b,a,c,d,e], [a,b,a]). then you must "extend" them and "insert" the element a in it, that is, the solution is to select all the elements that were not yet inserted, insert them all in a list with the element a for their head and discard them (for instance, [x,y,a,b,a]):
S={ [a,a], [a,b], [a,c], [a,d]}.
all_not_a([a|S],X).

Q:

A way to detect if a string contains an emoticon

The problem is that the following
if(string.IsNcLfLike('%\u0000\u0000%'))

does not seem to work. It works if you search for \u0000\u0000, but only if you search for the full string.
What I want to do is an emoticon that detects the occurrence of an emoticon in a string. The code doesn't work if you use \u0000\u0000\u0000

A:

If you want to detect the presence of a certain emoticon in a string, you can do this:
string text = "Hi, what's your name?";
string newText = text.Replace('%[^a-zA-Z\d]%', '$1');

That creates an entirely new text string containing the replacement, with the required text replaced by its contents.  The %[^a-zA-Z\d]% expression is just the opposite of the \U character class syntax in regexes.

You can use window functions if you create a table like this:
CREATE TABLE revenue
(
   Id INT IDENTITY
  , month int
  , TotalRevenue int
);

Then, you can use it in a sql statement like this:
SELECT
   month
  , SUM(TotalRevenue) OVER (Partition BY month)
FROM revenue
ORDER BY month;

The effects of chronic unpredictable stress on anxiety-related behaviors and monoamine neurotransmission in male mice.
Exposure to uncontrollable stress is a significant cause of anxiety and panic. Stress-induced anxiety disorders are the most common mental health disorders worldwide. This study examined the effects of chronic unpredictable stress (CUS) on anxiety-related behaviors and monoamine neurotransmitters in male mice. CUS was induced by unpredictable mild stress for 14 weeks. Mice in CUS group displayed increased anxiety-related behaviors, including anxiety index and increased immobility time in the forced swimming test. CUS mice showed significant decreases in dopamine (DA), and norepinephrine (NE) and serotonin (5-HT) levels. The results of the open field test and elevated plus maze test indicated that CUS mice had increased anxiety-related behaviors. The results from the Western blotting study revealed the protein expression of monoamine biosynthesis factors including tyrosine hydroxylase, and monoamine oxidase B (MAO) in the CUS group. These data suggested that CUS had marked effects on the monoamine content and the behavioral patterns in male mice.

Catheter or endocardial ablation has been used to treat a variety of cardiac arrhythmias. One approach involved direct current electrical cardioversion or defibrillation of the heart through the use of externally applied DC or radio frequency (RF) energy. In the other approach, a series of incisions or lesions were created on or in the myocardial tissue wall of the heart using a surgical procedure such as myctagon, which entails the incision of the tissues about the heart wall and suturing them to one another. Another more recent approach which was developed as an alternative to myocardial incision is described in U.S. Pat. No. 5,427,136, which issued Jun. 4, 1995.

A novel mechanism of endocarditis: the role of neutrophils.
Endocarditis is an uncommon and life-threatening complication of heart valve replacement surgery. It is also a common disease that results in considerable morbidity and mortality worldwide. An established cause factor for endocarditis is bacteremia, which stimulates cytokine expression in the blood, the cardiac tissue, and the circulating immune system. Despite the presence of this mechanism, only a small percentage of endocarditis cases have been reported to be bacteremic. In this paper our aim is to discuss whether patients with endocarditis have been under-recognized and under-treated. Further, we want to discuss whether it is possible that neutrophils, the most pathogenic immune cell of the blood circulation, might be directly involved in the pathogenesis of endocarditis. A literature review and a discussion regarding our results and limitations was performed.

Automated systems for detecting the presence or absence of a tooth abutment are used by dentists to avoid unnecessary procedures at the chairside. This study tested the effectiveness of an automated system designed for detecting the presence or absence of an abutment tooth and evaluated the dentists' performance. Two types of abutments were compared: fixed and telescoping. Five dentists (four male and one female) from four dental schools at three universities, each participated in this comparative study. The test was performed according to predefined criteria. Afterward, the data from the 5 dentists were analyzed as well as patient histories of all 16 patients who underwent abutment treatment. The dentists were asked to determine if abutments were present or absent when a dental impression or cone was made of a dental arch. The accuracy of the dentists in this study was 91.7% (91.7% of subjects were correctly diagnosed), and the sensitivity of the test was 84.4% (84.4% of subjects were correctly diagnosed as present when abutments were present). The development of microstructured optical fiber for polarization-independent telecommunications, such as dense-wavelength-division multiplexed communications, is the subject of intense scientific and engineering efforts due to their unique properties. In order to realize this goal, both fabrication and polarization-independent optical transmission of the fiber must be done in an integrated process in which the characteristics of each step of the process are carefully controlled. However, fiber performance strongly depends upon the properties of a substrate that carries the fiber structure. If the substrate does not have the desired optical features, the fiber can be made smaller (i.e., shorter) but only with higher levels of loss. Since the substrate is an integral part of the fiber, its performance must be maintained at the highest level consistent with manufacturability. In order to do this it is necessary to characterize the substrate, and to adjust its characteristics if necessary to meet the desired specifications. The optical fiber can also be made longer by adding additional waveguides to the fiber but this process will also reduce the coupling efficiency into the fiber core and will not be able to control fiber properties as effectively as simply fabricating longer fibers will.
An emerging technology for making optical components is based on the fabrication of the optical components on a flexible substrate. Examples

[Prevalence and prognosis of end-stage renal disease in the elderly: the influence of nephropathy, co-morbidity and treatment].
The most common causes of end-stage renal disease are diabetic nephropathy (40-50%), hypertensive nephropathy (20-25%) and glomerular diseases (5%). This paper examines the prevalence of these nephropathies in a representative, multifactorial sample drawn from a population of over 80000 persons, older than 65 years, who lived in Helsinki in 1985 (n = 5,233). The data on the prevalence and prognosis of renal disease in this population have been used to evaluate two hypotheses tested by statistical methods. We find that there is no difference in the prevalence of renal disease between old and young persons, but there are differences between groups on the presence of multiple morbidities. We conclude that although a large group of "old people with kidney disease" are in fact elderly persons with multiple morbidities, the prognosis is poor in both groups.

Q:

Does my custom module need .php in it?

Possible Duplicate:
Which extension should I use to write a PHP extension? 

Is it correct to install .php extensions in my custom module?
From what I understand, .php extensions (like mod_fcgid) will be auto-loaded, which will give me better performance.

A:

No, this is not necessary, only .htaccess or similar directives are needed. In order for Apache not to load PHP files as modules, so the only thing is to make sure apache loads some PHP modules:

Add the following line to your [httpd.conf] section
LoadModule rewrite_module modules/mod_rewrite.so

And make sure you have the module in your mod_php

So the only thing needed is that you place a PHP file in your module in the corresponding php.ini file, with the right permissions so your apache knows what to do with the PHP file/module.

In January I wrote a piece for the London Review of Books in which I described my life as a gay man in London at the turn of the 21st century. I’d been born and brought up by gay parents in West Bromwich in the West Midlands and when I was four my mother discovered my sexual orientation and the rest is history.



It’s worth repeating. I was born in West Bromwich and brought up in West Bromwich, a place which had become the cultural capital of the south-west Midlands in the 1970s. The place was full of gay people who had decided to move away from the traditional areas of the city and the Midlands – the suburbs that had been full of them a generation earlier.

At that time there was still a lot of homophobia in Britain. The only reason I got away with a straight father who was a police officer was because my mother – also a middle class Londoner from an upper class family – was pretty sure that anyone with a police background was pretty sure to have a happy life: not so much because she agreed with the attitude, but because she knew that the policeman would get them out of the town easily, where the police were more likely to harass them and get things changed around

In vitro and in vivo studies have shown that oral administration of high dose of gentamicin (80mg/kg bodyweight) induces renal damage in humans. The present study attempted to define the time of onset of pharmacokinetic/pharmacological/clinical alterations induced by high dose gentamicin in rats. The present study also attempted to identify and confirm renal damage in this model. Oral gentamicin therapy in rats was carried out for 3 days. Blood and urine samples were taken to determine gentamicin concentrations using gas chromatography. Serum urea, creatinine and uric acid were determined using commercial kits. Renal function was determined by measuring the kidney (nephrons/100 ml) and bladder weights, by determining the concentration of urea and creatinine in urine. Kidney damage was confirmed by histopathological examination of renal tissues. Blood and urine creatinine levels and urinary uric acid levels were increased after high dose gentamicin therapy. Kidney (nephrons/100 ml) and bladder tissues (g/100 g) were decreased to 46.43 and 20.03 respectively in control group while kidney tissues and bladder tissues decreased to 52.13 and 22.02 respectively in gentamicin treated rats.

In a major expansion, the U.S. House approved the Trans-Pacific Partnership (TPP) on Friday despite widespread opposition.

The House vote came on the cusp of a possible U.S. government shutdown, which is not an option in the House and one that Republicans, who control the House, are unlikely to accept.

It was also just hours after the two-year-old Trade and Development Partnership would expire.

President Barack Obama called on Congress to approve the Pacific Rim trade deal during a telephone conversation with King of Thailand H.E. King Maha Vajiralongkorn — the first time the U.S. has used the term “King” in its diplomatic communications.

“We will come back in two weeks. It’s a very long process. We have to do it right,” Obama stated Friday.

While the House vote came out of the blue, other congressional resolutions on the TPP were passed before Obama had had a chance to speak with King on June 25.

According to a congressional source speaking on condition of anonymity, the House votes were necessary before another House vote could occur to extend a budget bill set to expire on the 30th.

Bilirubin, a marker of neonatal jaundice, reduces DNA-repair activity and increases mutation and DNA breakage in a human cell model.
Exposure to newborn jaundice results in increased levels of bilirubin in plasma. Plasma bilirubin and unconjugated bilirubin levels during the first days of life were determined in 36 newborn infants presenting with various degrees of jaundice. These infants had their bilirubin levels determined every other day during their first month of life. The effects of bilirubin exposure on DNA-repair activity, on DNA-mutations, as well as on DNA strand breakage were evaluated. An interesting finding was that even a moderate increase in plasma bilirubin concentration during the first few days of life significantly reduced DNA-repair activity and increased mutation and DNA breakage, when cells were exposed to the compound for 48 hours. However, the effect of bilirubin was more pronounced during the second week (with a maximum reduction of 50% of the DNA-repair activity in the group receiving bilirubin during the initial 30 hours of life and 50% during the last 30 hours).

Q:

Does the Cauchy sequence exist?

I've seen that Cauchy sequence $x(n)$ does not exist and that is the reason why I'm trying to prove it. Is it enough to find counterexample with infinite terms? But how?

A:

Yes, it is enough.
Suppose the Cauchy sequence does not have exactly $n$ terms. What can you get out of a contradiction? We need to find a number $N$ such that $|x(n)|\geq n!$ for $n>N$.
Now take $N=\prod_{k=2}^\infty k!$, the product of all products of powers of $2$. This is larger than $\sum_n n!={\displaystyle{\frac{1}{1-1}}}$ as $n!$ is between $1$ and $2$, so $|x(n)|\geq n!>N$ which is a contradiction since we assumed that $|x(n)|\leq n!$ for $n>N$.

[Risk factors for and prognosis of ischemic stroke in elderly patients aged 80 years or over].
Stroke rates are generally the highest in the elderly, and ischemic stroke in that age group has often a very poor prognosis. The purpose of this study was to investigate risk factors for and prognosis of ischemic stroke in elderly Japanese patients. A total of 1,014 consecutive patients who had undergone computed tomography angiography (CTA) for the first or second time between June 2009 and May 2010, and who survived, were enrolled in this retrospective observational study. Age (<70 years: n=862; 70-79 years: n=93) or age 80 years or over (n=19) was the only significant factor between those showing and not showing early staining on the CTA findings. Patients who were 80 years or over had significantly more high-risk comorbidity, e.g., heart failure, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and depression, and less life expectancy.

1. The Field of the Invention
The present invention pertains to network devices and processing systems having a distributed control architecture. More particularly, this invention relates to a network switch with a distributed network processing system.
2. Background
The growing amount of data being handled by modern computer systems has created a demand for more sophisticated network devices capable of handling large amounts of data at high speed. Network systems may employ hubs or trunk line links to allow a single point-of-presence to communicate all the communication traffic among multiple computers or workstations. This arrangement often presents several drawbacks including an increased cost of interconnection between the point-of-presence and the remote computers or workstations.
Traditionally, a network switches, such as those used to interconnect computers, required a great deal of memory and substantial processing resources to control the flow of message traffic through its internal routing system. This is particularly common when the network switches support a relatively large number of interfaces, such as is the case in a telecommunications environment employing a variety of protocols to handle the transmission of voice, data, and video communications.
More recently, distributed processor-based network switches have been proposed as a cost-effective solution for handling large amounts of message traffic. These switches,

This invention relates, in general, to the field of software design and, more specifically, to the design of a database that is capable of storing information on multiple objects in a modular manner.
2. Description of the Related Art
Software developers design software applications for the needs of the market. This requires the software developers to determine what software application can be used and to determine how the software application should be used.
Once the needs and desires of the market have been evaluated, the next step is to create a software design. This is done by first designing the object model and then by determining the relationship between objects.
There are multiple ways to represent the data relationships between objects into the software design. One approach is to use a database management system, such as a relational database management system (RDBMS) or a “data warehouse”. A database in such a system allows multiple users to have access to their objects stored in the database but is not always able to provide all the functionality that is required. Bidirectional communication between a neuron and glial cells in mammals.
Molecular and cellular communication between cells in the central nervous system are generally thought to involve the release of neuromodulators in single direction on a short time scale (1-3 min) while the transmission of synaptic information on long time scales (hours). However, a long history of the physiological study of bidirectional interactions between astrocytes and neurons has shown that the glial cells can also participate in the regulation of neuronal function through the release of neurotransmitters and neuro-active agents. In this review we will report the most recent knowledge concerning the communication process between astrocytes and neurons in the brain and suggest how it can be regulated to modulate the function of the nervous system. The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services' Office of Refugee Resettlement has announced that the government's processing of refugee applications has surpassed the goal of ending refugee family separations at the U.S. border. As a result, it will no longer hold unaccompanied minors in detention, as HHS stated that was the goal two years ago.

"We're not closing the door on reunifying all of the children who were separated for more than a year at the border," said Health and Human Services Secretary Alex Azar in a news conference Wednesday. "We're still going to be processing applications where there are children who need to be identified within 48 hours and are going to be returned to the home countries, where they face dangers as a result of coming into the country illegally."

Azar explained that HHS is no longer holding children in immigration detention facilities while they await the reunification of their families. The agency's goal for reunifying unaccompanied minors was to hold them in detention until the government determines whether it will accept them. The cell surface receptor protein tyrosine kinases (PTKs) comprise a large family of kinases involved in the control and signalling pathways that control cell growth and differentiation. Members of the PTK family contain several unique and conserved domains. The most conserved domain in PTKs is the catalytic domain, which in the PTKs is unique from other eukaryotic Ser/Threonine kinases. The catalytic domain catalyzes covalent transfer of the phosphate from adenosine triphosphate (ATP) to a tyrosine residue in the protein substrate. PTKs can be divided into two classes, receptor-like and nonreceptor-like. Receptor-like tyrosine kinases have an extracellular, a transmembrane and an intracellular domain comprising, from the N-terminus, typically seven, repeated sequences of about 100 to 104 amino acids. The nonreceptor-like tyrosine kinases are intramembranous and lack significant homology with members of any other family of protein kinase.

In this review, we discuss the use of proteomic approaches to investigate tumor growth and tumor invasion - two fundamental steps in tumorigenesis. Proteomic approaches are particularly useful for biomarker discovery to support clinical decision-making in oncology. We describe the various proteomic technologies available, and highlight the potential clinical implications of these technologies in the setting of cancer treatment and surveillance. We also discuss proteomic biomarkers for cancer prognosis and for response to oncological treatment.

Gut microbiota and cancer risk: the roles of diet, alcohol, and tobacco consumption.
The concept of microbiota in the development of complex diseases such as cancer has been increasingly recognized. Cancer development has many known contributing factors and it is a complex and lengthy process. Gut microbiota plays an important role in the development of different cancer types, and may promote cancer growth, progression, and progression. Dietary habits are strongly associated with microbiota composition and vice versa. Alcohol, tobacco, and a vegetarian diet are frequently consumed in conjunction with specific gut microbiota and dietary habits could also greatly influence microbiota composition. We summarize evidence on the association between diet, gut microbiota, and cancer development in the context of evidence published on how diet influences gut microbiota in specific gut microbiota-affected organs such as the liver, gallbladder and pancreas. Furthermore, evidence is provided that suggests the possibility that smoking and alcohol consumption may also influence certain types of cancer, either alone or in correlation with diet and microbiota.

Surgical management of hepatocellular carcinoma: current status and review of the literature.
The current treatment of non-cirrhotic hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is surgical resection with curative intent. While the long-term prognosis for patients with early stage and small HCC has not significantly changed over the last decade, the prognosis for patients with more advanced (T3-T4), non-curative liver resections (hepatic resections with or without portal vein embolization, hepatic arterial infusion chemotherapy) is dismal, with a very high risk of recurrence. The reasons for this dismal outcome are multifactorial and include the effect of surgical resection, the development of intrahepatic or distant metastases, as well as the postoperative recurrence of HCC. Currently, there are no reliable predictors other than the Barcelona Clinic of Liver Cancer (BCLC) criteria for the selection of patients most likely to benefit from liver resection. The BCLC staging system should remain the benchmark for patient selection for non-curative operations for HCC until such time as validated predictors for non-curative resections prove to be available.

Mapping of three-dimensional protein structures into the electron microscopy map of yeast ribosomes.
We have produced two 3-dimensional reconstructions of the entire yeast ribosome in order to study the organization of the ribosomal subunits. The model of the entire mitochondrial ribosome is based on a series of four X-ray diffraction images of a crystal produced from a yeast mitochondrially reconstituted in a liposome membrane. The structural model of the yeast mitochondrial ribosome agrees well with the electron microscopic information obtained for the same organelle. The structural model for the ribosome in Escherichia coli cells is produced by the same procedure as that used in the reconstruction of the yeast ribosome. The yeast ribosome is modeled in its native form, in contrast to the ribosomal subunits of many mammalian ribosomes which are considered as crystalline in nature. As a result of a combination of the X-ray diffraction data from yeast ribosomes and the electron microscope images from Escherichia coli ribosomes, the ribosome structures are modeled from four images of a yeast ribosome and six images of Escherichia coli ribosomes.

Wigford is a civil parish in the Borough of Stafford, Staffordshire, England. It contains two settlements, Wigford and West Wigford, both of which are also in Staffordshire.  The village in the centre of the parish is called Wigford, and the hamlet in the east is called West Wigford. The church was a gift from John Marshall, 1st Baron of Bletchingley, and has a well-appointed chancel and a large tower. In this article, we examined the relationship between antithrombotic drug use and the incidence of hemorrhagic stroke in acute ischemic stroke (AIS) patients. A total of 472 patients, who were divided into 2 groups according to antithrombotic drugs used, were included. The study population consisted of patients aged 65 years or older, with a National Institutes of Health Stroke Scale Score of 9 or higher and no previous history of hemorrhagic stroke or intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH). After a mean follow-up time of 4.6 years, the incidence of hemorrhagic stroke was 7.3%. The occurrence rates of hemorrhagic stroke in the first, second, and third year of stroke development were 10.0%, 8.3%, and 6.4%, respectively. The patients using antithrombotics before discharge were not at higher risk, in terms of incidence of hemorrhagic stroke. Q:

Why don't we need to use the "=" sign when assigning values to the C array?

In C language, there are two possible ways to assign values to the variable a, and two different ways to assign array values to the variable a when the variable name is "=" instead of being in the array:
a = 0
a=0

Why are these two ways not identical? Why don't we use the "=" sign when assigning values to a variable?

A:

If you assign value to C-array (for example arr[0]) with arr = 2; it is considered as array initialization.
If you assign value to C-array with arr[=2] then it is considered as assignment with expression.
Both ways of initialization produce exactly same result because you can't assign values to arrays that are more then size one. So why one can't use  the = operator to assign values to first argument of function call?
If compiler is smart enough to analyze both methods correctly then it will produce exactly same results without any differences.

A novel method for the isolation and culture of human hepatocytes from the liver tissue removed for surgery.
In order to develop a new method for isolation and culture of human hepatocytes from a surgical specimen, we used a three-step method in which the liver tissue was first washed, then treated with collagenase, and finally seeded with hepatocytes that were isolated from the wash solution by centrifugation. This newly developed method gave rise to a 100-fold increase in the rate of hepatocyte isolation compared with the conventional method of tissue homogenization or centrifugation for cell detachment. We further confirmed the ability of the method to allow for the attachment of hepatocytes to plastic culture dishes by observing the formation of a monolayer of hepatocytes cultured with rat hepatoblastoma cells (NIT-1) on these dishes. Furthermore, we showed that human hepatocytes obtained from the isolated cells after three steps of the method retained their ability to proliferate as a monolayer culture in culture for 5 to 7 days. In conclusion, the three-step method described here is a highly reliable and reproducible method for the isolation and culturing of hepatocytes from the liver tissue of surgical patients.

Degradation of human red cell membrane glycoproteins during storage under various conditions.
It has been suggested that exposure of red cells to osmotic stress or elevated temperatures and other conditions known to cause cell swelling and shrinkage might lead to the destruction or modification of membrane glycoproteins. To study the effects of the physiological conditions on the membrane glycoproteins, the red cell membranes were subjected to heat treatment. Heat treatment affected the degree of phosphatidylserine (PS)-decoration but not the content of PS. The incubation of the red cell membranes with glycosidase produced a further increase in the degree of PS-decoration. The results were consistent with the changes in phospholipids during the incubation of the red cell membranes with trypsin, N-acetyl-D-glucosaminidase or N-acetyl-D-galactosaminidase. These observations suggest that red cell membrane glycoproteins are resistant to the physiological conditions caused by temperature stress and osmotic stress and to glycosidase in normal or pathological conditions.

Non-dispersive optical spectroscopy systems measure a property of an object by measuring the intensity of a light reflected back from the illuminated object according to a well known principle of the interference of light waves (e.g., light originating from the object being tested and from a reference light source). In conventional non-dispersive optical spectroscopy systems, a detector measures the intensity of a reflected light of the light wave received from the object. The non-dispersive optical spectroscopy systems may also be used to measure the polarization of the reflected light, i.e., the polarization state of the reflected light may be different from a polarization state of an incident light wave.

The survey of more than 3,500 people conducted by Sky also revealed that six out of 10 of those polled stated that they are still undecided on whether they actually would choose to live on another planet.

The other 24 per cent, however, claim they would never consider going anywhere else in the universe.

Of the people polled, one per cent would rather be in a submarine.

The poll also found that two out of five people polled actually would consider moving home to another planet if they possessed the money and decided to do so. Thirty-six per cent of respondents said they believe that the world is in danger after the rise of the internet and the spread of cybercrime.

In March a survey conducted by a UK organisation revealed that just over a fifth of people said they would like to move to another world, despite having a belief in alien life.

Another poll conducted for the Huffington Post suggests that there are a few things that make humans more likely to be killed in a car crash than the planet Earth.

Q:

How do I calculate this sum of log

How do I calculate this sum of the log function?
I would like to understand from where this comes from.
$$\log \left(\frac{z}{1-z}\right) = \int_0^{+\infty} \frac{\log(1+s)}{s}ds $$
$$\frac{\log(z)}{(-z)^{-1}} = -z + \sum_{n = 1}^{\infty} \frac{\log(z + n)}{z + n}$$

A:

There is a lot of stuff here, but a simpler answer is that
$$\log \frac{a}{b} = \log a - \log b + i \arg (b)$$
where $i$ the square root of $-1$. We can find the logarithm of $\frac{z}{1 - z}$ by multiplying with $s = \frac{z}{1 - z}$.  Then we have the sum to integrate over the entire complex plane.

In fact it is true that $i \arg (x)$ is the log

A woman was stabbed Saturday in the back by an off-duty officer in South Los Angeles, a police spokesman said.

The victim, believed to be in her 20s, was walking near MacArthur Park with a friend when she was attacked in the 1200 block of East Cesar Chavez about 12:25 a.m., Det. Chris Martinez of the LAPD said.

When she tried to defend herself, the officer grabbed her head and stabbed her in the back, Martinez said. The victim ran to the street and was hit by a taxi.


The woman was rushed to a hospital while her friend was taken to jail on suspicion of assault with a deadly weapon, police said. She was released about 11:30 a.m. She was not injured.

She could not be reached for comment Saturday night.

Police described the off-duty officer as “wearing black Nike sneakers.”

“The officer is not facing any disciplinary action,” Martinez said.

Police are investigating whether the attack was motivated by racism. The victim was Asian, police said.


It was the third stabbing Saturday morning in downtown South Los Angeles. One man, 48, was slashed in the back with a knife

Q:

How many prime numbers are there less than $x$?

How many prime numbers are there less than $x$: $x<1000$?

A:

This is known as Lagrange's theorem, and has an elementary proof (see e.g. this MO question).
In fact the number of primes $\le t$ is the $n$th harmonic number 
$$H_n = \frac1{n!}\left(1 - \frac1n\right)^n.$$
You are looking for $H_n$ in the domain where $n<x$. We see that $$H_n = n + (n-1)!\,\zeta(n),$$
where $\zeta$ is Euler's constant. (You may find some good explanation of why $H_n$ is related to zeta in this Wikipedia page). (I have written this down in my own words here for convenience of presentation.) 
The formula $H_n = n + (n-1)!\,\zeta(n)$ was actually derived already in 1859, by the great German mathematician Joseph von-Helmholtz

Q:

MySQL: Find matching records based on one field and the other

I have 2 separate tables in my database: one with events and one with guests.
I would like to get a table of guests where in the event the guest have the same status of the event and in the event there is an event that the guest has to go there. 
table entries would be:
GuestID   GuestType    GuestStatus
1         1            1
2         2            2

table entries would be:
EventID   Status    Guests
1         1         1, 2
2         2         3

I have tried a where but it return multiple rows. Is possible to get one row?

A:

You need a join:
SELECT EventID, Status AS EventStatus, Guests 
FROM Events e
INNER JOIN Guests g

As is known in the art, optical fibers are hollow tubes that are produced through a chemical etching process and can be used for optical transmission. The optical fibers usually consist of a metal core covered with a protective, transparent outer coating. A typical composition of the coating for an optical fiber is an organic fluoropolymer material which shows substantially higher optical clarity and strength than that of an organic polymer having a comparable refractive index.
Optical fiber couplers, also referred to as optical fiber multiplexers, are optical devices for converting the light signals that can traverse one optical fiber into a light signal that can traverse at least one other optical fiber. It is often important that optical fiber couplers have a fairly broad bandwidth to facilitate the transmission of large amounts of data and avoid crosstalk between different signals of the same kind.
Optical fiber couplers used in wavelength division multiplexing (WDM) optical fiber communication networks and the like are generally of the optical fiber directional coupler type using a biconical-optical-fiber-over-singlemode-fiber or "BOSF" as a reflecting element with two optical fibers, e.g. as shown in FIG. 9, wherein the singlemode guide fiber

The theme for the Rural Women of Tomorrow’s year is ‘Connecting and Connecting to’, and the organisers have chosen the story of the ‘Catch’ women as a model for women of the future generations in rural and urban areas in Australia and New Zealand.

The Catch woman story is the story of a young mother who ran an illegal wool farm in rural New South Wales while her family struggled financially and with the loss of their home. Her courage and conviction resulted in her being arrested, fined and jailed for 13 months due to the public outrage she caused.

The story of the ‘Catch’ woman is a story about courage and perseverance. She had the courage to take on a difficult and uncertain life and she used this courage to forge a path through adversity and to become the successful business woman she is today. The Catch story is a story about a young woman who refused to be trapped in the cycle of poverty that has been visited on her family. She had the courage to go out into the business world and pursue her dream.

The European Union was formed in 1957. Since then many different nations have joined the bloc, and now it also comprises 25 member states from the former Soviet Union and its eastern members. But the EU itself is a largely loose organization – one of the largest political and economic cooperation blocs formed without a single formal political treaty.

Over the past 70 years, these 25 independent nations and bloc have signed hundreds of treaties with only a handful of others involved.

The EU is a largely invisible and largely powerless player in the world, at least at any one global level. As a free trade bloc, it isn’t even a real international trade organization — the EU simply deals with trade issues that are on a regional basis. As an international organization, EU member states have no influence over decisions by, or control over, the organization as a whole either.

This is how the EU is made up and has remained for decades, and the EU’s lack of organization over its member states means that its members are only indirectly represented in international political decisions and in international economic negotiations.

Semiconductor packaging technology is well known in the industry as a method for protecting and interconnecting semiconductor devices. A typical semiconductor package includes one or more dies, or chips, and one or more leads adapted to be electrically connected with the devices on the chips.
One type of semiconductor packaging technology is the Ball Grid Array (“BGA”) package in which a die of the die stack is coupled to a lead frame and a semiconductor die of the die stack is encapsulated in a molding compound or molded epoxy. The lead frame and semiconductor die may be placed on a printed circuit board, and the BGA package may then be connected to the printed circuit board by using solder balls or bumps.
Another type of semiconductor packaging technology is the flip chip or Controlled Collapse Chip Connection (C4) package in which a semiconductor die of the die stack is electrically connected to a die pad or a lead frame via a solder ball connection.

Q:

How to delete multiple items from dictionary in python

There's a dictionary that each key can have at most 100 elements. What I want to do is remove all the items in the dictionary where the number of elements is less than the 100 limit.
I tried this:
maxCount=100
dictionary.get(number, 0)

A:

You can use the max(iterable) function to set the value of the maxCount at the first place of the iterable. Then with the use of defaultdict() you can use the built-in method items() to access them as list. If you want the item at the key position, the index is provided.
>>> maxCount = 100
>>> defaultdict(lambda:0, max(range(maxCount)))
defaultdict(<function <lambda> at 0x10c1e1ef8>), {0: 100}

Note that with the defaultdict(lambda:0) call you set the value at the first position of the defaultdict with the max(range(maxCount)) call which means that in all other positions, the maxCount will be zero.

The effect of temperature on growth and the activity of Escherichia coli O157:H7 in meat products.
The inactivation of Escherichia coli O157:H7 grown in meat extracts is reported for three temperature levels. The relationship between survival and time under refrigeration and meat storage was investigated. Two types of meat were analysed (cold-cut/hamburg) using two storage temperatures (4 and 15 degrees C; 4 degrees C in product); two levels of meat extract (30 and 60 g/kg). The effects of temperature, storage and storage time on survival of E. coli O157:H7 were investigated. E. coli O157:H7 was inactivated after 20 days of refrigeration at 25 degrees C. Increasing storage time enhanced the inactivation effect, especially in the cold-cut meat. After 4 days of refrigeration time, E. coli O157:H7 was detected in the meat extracts stored in a refrigerated room with low temperature (4 degrees C and 15 degrees C) whereas no detection was observed in extracts stored at a refrigerated room with high temperature (4 degrees C and 15 degrees C + 60 g/kg meat extract).

Craniofacial anomalies: evaluation by cone beam computed tomography (CBCT).
In this study, we evaluated craniofacial anomalies using cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) as an alternative option to plain radiography for initial imaging. The study included 55 cases of children ranging from 1 to 16 years. The patients underwent CBCT studies of various head conditions, followed by an intra-oral examination to detect developmental dysplasia of the hip, talipes equino varus or varus, or scoliosis. CBCT showed the following skeletal anomalies in all cases: 1) hypoplasia of the lateral pterygoid muscles, 2) hypoplasia of the transverse mandibular ligament, and 3) subluxation of the distal part of the hyoid bone. In addition, we observed the following conditions with CBCT: 1) talipes equino varus in 3 patients, 2) malocclusion of the maxilla without malformative signs in 8 patients, 3) asymmetry in the craniofacial skeleton in 4 patients, and 4) the presence of other dental anomalies in 2 patients. In conclusion, CBCT can serve to determine the exact location and extent of abnormalities in the cran

Papua New Guinea's High Court has released two young men who went missing in the last fortnight following the abduction by suspected Islamic militants for ransom of their brother and former president of the country, according to police.

The two men - both aged 29 - had gone missing on August 30 during a mission to their home village in Lofung Province about 1,000 kilometres (620 miles) east of the capital,

They were kidnapped while on the way to the airport to attend the funeral of a young relative, according to police.

They are reported to still be in police custody.

According to a local media report, the younger of two missing brothers reported that he was abducted by suspected Muslim extremist rebels from his village while he was at the airport to attend the funeral.

He was then held to ransom for $500 and has been in police custody since.

Papua New Guinea is a predominantly Muslim country of over 200,000 people who practice the Muslim religion of Islam.

Efficacy of ciprofloxacin in the treatment of patients with chronic osteomyelitis and prosthetic joint infection.
Several antibiotic therapies have been suggested for use in the treatment of chronic osteomyelitis and prosthetic infection and this study examined the efficacy of ciprofloxacin against the organisms involved in these chronic infections. Twenty consecutive patients with chronic osteomyelitis and prosthetic hip and knee infections were treated with ciprofloxacin (a loading dose of 500 mg plus a daily maintenance dose of 250 mg) for 4 to 8 weeks. Twenty age-matched patients with chronic osteomyelitis without prosthetic infection were treated with doxycycline at the same dose for 4 weeks as a control group to assess the effect of doxycycline on bone healing. The bacteria isolated from the chronic osteomyelitis and prosthetic joint infections were: coagulase-negative staphylococci 12, Gram-negative cocci 10, Staphylococcus epidermidis 2, Staphylococcus aureus 2, and Bacillus subtilis 6. The organisms isolated in the chronic osteomyelitis and prosthetic joint infections were mostly sensitive to cipro

As a young woman, I worked with dozens of people, many of whom were children, in a non-profit organization for low-income young women to come to the United States with their undocumented parents as undocumented young adult women to live as full citizens. As a result of all of my interactions with the children, I knew that I hated the idea one minute and then loved the idea the next. I knew that my feelings were often conflicted and that they changed with each individual child’s situation.

However, what I came to realize after my first year working with the organization is that what we call “children” are in reality children of war. As my time with the organization had passed, I continued to get more of a sense of this warring in me with each child, with each family, and with each person we were working with. Through this, it became increasingly clear to me that not only do we not know the reasons why children fight in wars, we also do not know why children fight in wars of other children, and we are also not aware of how our experiences can inform our relationships with each other or how other children can enrich our experiences.

Pilot Study of a Home-Based Intervention Using Computer and Mobile Communications Technology to Improve Weight Loss in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.
To compare home-based (HB) interventions using mobile and computer communication technology (ICT) to a HB intervention that provided no ICT for weight-loss intervention, in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM). Prospective, randomized, single-blind, 12-month, parallel group pilot with concealed allocation, two-group superiority pre-post intervention, and one-month follow-up, of a total 60 patients. Intervention comprised three 1-month, HB phases and a 6-month, computer intervention phase. Patients provided daily self-monitoring of their weight and HbA1c over 18 months and answered questions about the HB intervention. Primary outcomes were proportion of patients reaching goal HbA1c, frequency of HbA1c measurement, and changes in weight and waist circumference. Participants were also assessed for adherence and adverse events. [Antitumor activity of liposomes containing a new derivative of chloromycetin].
The antitumor activity of liposomes containing chloromycetin derivative and phosphatidylcholines (PC) was explored in rats with a transplanted sarcoma after treatment with a single dose of chloromycetin (50 mg/kg). The effect of the lipid membrane on PC incorporation as well as on the phospholipase A2-catalysed hydrolysis of PC and the possible mechanisms of chloromycin action on sarcoma cells were shown. The most effective antitumor dose of chloromycetin was used in the study. In comparison with free drug the liposomal chloromycin, liposome membrane cholesterol, PC and a combination of liposome membrane and PC with the maximum effect on sarcoma growth inhibition were obtained. The possible mechanisms of the antitumor activity of chloromycin are discussed.

I was very curious about the last part of the blog post “Dying to Know” because I had never thought about dying before. Acute renal failure is rarely caused by neoplasms involving the kidney or the lower tract of the ureter. Acute renal failure due to urological cancers may be caused by malignant tumors of the lower urinary tract, ureter, kidney, bladder, or the upper urinary tract; renal artery aneurysms; or renal artery occlusion. The University of Texas at Austin is using a powerful technology developed by its engineering students to determine what lies within the Earth's mantle, a rock layer lying between the continents.

That's a massive change from the U.S. Geological Survey, which uses seismology and computer models to map the subsurface of the globe.

The University of Texas, a major player in the United States, wants to know what it is beneath Texas.

The technology the engineering students developed using lasers and a new tool has allowed scientists to create the first complete 3D maps of the Earth's subsurface mantle, which sits between the deep hot fluid ocean and the cold dry continental crust.

The UT Austin work in Texas could have implications for understanding the origins of our planet's magnetic field.

UT Austin is one of the nation's top public research universities. It is part of the University of Texas at Austin, the fifth-oldest university in Texas.

The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit, in California, struck down California’s $1.15 fee-for-service billing system, which was also in place at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas. In a 2-1 decision, the appeals court found that the fees were “not uniformly based on actual hours,” but instead were based on the average number of hours billed by each attorney.

One reason the court said the fees were so low was because those lawyers who had previously spent the most hours on a class action, could have been paid “a flat fee, not proportionate to the hours worked on the litigation,” a decision that will have some reverberations inside the federal judiciary as judges across the country face similar questions.

The National Arbitration Forum of Washington state, which represents many large businesses on arbitration and mediation practices and is supported by nearly 2,000 arbitrators, is also considering whether to pursue similar lawsuits.

What happens if a federal judge upholds the $1.15 per hour fee, as many judges have suggested? One could argue he or she will be the first federal judge in history to decide a class action case on class action fees.

B. Description of the Prior Art
Polymerization of olefins to make olefin polymers such as high density polyethylene (HDPE) typically involves the use of Ziegler-Natta catalysts supported on the surface of silica. Such polymerization processes have the advantage of high polymerization rates but their operating conditions are limited by the availability of commercially-viable Ziegler-Natta catalysts. This limitation has been overcome by the use of supported metal complexes which are characterized by very high activity and high catalyst-to-polymer ratios. However, the catalyst efficiency of these techniques is typically low as compared to the much higher activity and high catalyst-to-polymer ratio of single site catalysts and homogeneous polymerization techniques. The participants were followed-up daily for a total duration of 28 days. The primary endpoint was the proportion of participants with parasite clearance. This type of interaction between individual computers, is termed peer-to-peer.
A peer-to-peer application, which is in addition is being used also for the internet, is an application that permits a peer to connect directly with another peer computer, where each peer has an individualized connection, i.e. the peer connections are different. That is to say, one peer is communicating directly with another peer, while the second peer is communicating with yet another peer. Thus, where there is a large number of peers, in fact, thousands or possibly millions of peer-to-peers are connected to each other through peer-to-peer applications.
Such peer-to-peer applications are useful for a number of different reasons. Firstly, a user can more easily communicate with different applications, which provide information to the user, than would be possible using a user who communicates with other applications via their own desktop programs. The New York Times has a profile on the late actor and comedian Robert Downey Jr., and while I don’t really care to cover celebrities, I do get the occasional oddball who makes an interesting case, and Robert Downey Jr. made a great case. Downey Jr. is perhaps best known for his role as the villain on the TV series Miami Vice, but in addition to being an actor, he is also a writer, director, and actor-producer. He wrote, directed, produced, and starred in the film The Horse Whisperer, which was released later that year. A few months before that film was released, Downey released his third book, The Art of Manliness: How to Succeed in the Real World (Knopf, 1987). This is not, I assume, a book about what it takes to be an awesome dude or how to survive as a gay man. This is just a book that is actually about how to do exactly the things that a person who is an awesome dude might do. In other words, the book is about manliness. It’s great, and it’s definitely one I would recommend to other men in all phases of their lives.

Prayer of Saint Francis of Assisi

The Prayer of Saint Francis of Assisi (Latin: Pontificale S. Francis d'Assisi, or Psofas da S. Francesco di Assisi) is a prayer of Saint Francis of Assisi. It is usually called the "Prisoner's Prayer"; it consists in three main parts: I. Praying for God to deliver the prisoner. II. For God to let him die unshaken, without anger. (This latter petition can be used as a sort of "crowning grace"; the third part, in which the prayer ends, is repeated five times, and then the prayer begins.)

A variation appears in the Catechism of the Catholic Church: the prayer may be repeated five times, and may conclude with five psalms (Psalm 130) or one psalm.

This prayer is also said after the catechism by some theologians; it is often said in the churches of England and Ireland, in addition to its traditional use, to assist individuals or groups with an emotional or spiritual need in praying the Angelus. The role of psoriasis as a risk factor for cerebrovascular disease mortality is unclear. We performed a case-control study to explore these issues. We linked the national healthcare databases of the British Columbia (BC) Ministry of Health and the British Columbia Stroke Registry to identify death records of patients with definite cases of cerebral infarction (ICD-9/10 codes 430-438) and non-causal controls (ICD-9/10 codes 290-294, 531-538) who were matched on age on December 31, 1998, to the index date of each definite case. During the 2-year follow-up period, there were 692 definite cases of cerebral infarction among 11,200 patients with psoriasis and 16,100 matched controls.
